JP.filename: 3.23, Weekly List for John Paul McAdams.pdf
JP.raw_text:
Weekly List for John Paul McAdamsMonday, March 23, 2026Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Lesson 63Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 1Work for 5 minutes reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Also review any of the questions for this year thatyou find you need to work on. Read Chapter 16 of Our Life in the Church.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 3Go over workbook page 121 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 122-123. (T.E. pages 243-247.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 1Mini Review Lessons 4-6, Selection 30: write the selection correctly.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Write sentences using the Lesson 8 Key words. You can put each word in a separate sentence, but if you use more than one word inthe same sentence that is even better. Make sure your sentences are intelligible.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 3Lesson 22. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 1Do some Nature Journaling.History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Read pp. 187-197 in The Old World and America. Do the Objective Tests, giving the source of your answer. If the answer comes outof the text, give the page number on which it is found. If you learned the answer from a different source, cite that source. Then dofive of the Questions that Make You Think and three of the Questions that Test Your Character on pp. 195-196. Remember, eachanswer should be a short paragraph that is three or more sentences long and should include the page number where you found theinformation. [Please note that this is an assessment assignment. Tips on completing these questions are available to parents in thesyllabus resources: Old World & America Questions - Tips and Instructions for Assessments.]Assessment Assignment:Week 16, Day 2, Questions That Make You Think & Questions That Test Your Character (pp. 195-196)3/22/26, 6:37 PMPage 1 of 7
   Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 3Memorize and review for five minutes.Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 13, Day: 5Optional: Do Art Pad #13, Baroque Garland Project, Part 1.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 1Read for about 1 hour.3/22/26, 6:37 PMPage 2 of 7
  
        Tuesday, March 24, 2026Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 4Lesson 64Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Work for 5 minutes reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Also review any of the questions for this year thatyou find you need to work on. Write out the spiritual and corporal works of mercy. Discuss Chapter 16 of Our Life in the Church withyour teacher using the discussion questions in Chapter 16 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources).The exercises could be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions inChapter 16, Lessons 1-5 of the activity book as a guide.)English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 4Do workbook page 124. Then go over workbook pages 125-127 with your teacher. (T.E. pages 248-253.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 2Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: find the errors and mark them in your book.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 3Write a single paragraph using as many of the Lessons 7 and 8 Key Words as you can. Make sure the paragraph is intelligible.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 4Lesson 22. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 2Read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.1.History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 3Start reading The Magna Charta by James Daugherty (in print). Read it for the next week. If you find yourself with more time, try TheRed Keep and The Lost Baron by Allen French, or The Hidden Treasure of Glaston by Eleanor M. Jewett (all in print).Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 4Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 2Read for about 1 hour.3/22/26, 6:37 PMPage 3 of 7
   
      Wednesday, March 25, 2026Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 5Test 15Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 3Work for 5 minutes reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Also review any of the questions for this year that youfind you need to work on. Add the spiritual and corporal works of mercy to your memory work.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 5Complete English 8: Semesterly Assessment 1 online.Assessment Assignment:English 8: Semesterly Assessment 1Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 3Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: write the selection correctly.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 4Take a spelling test on the Lesson 7 and Lesson 8 Key Words.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 5Lesson 22. Take Quiz 22.Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 3Re-read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.1. Answer question 7.1 and The Big Idea in your workbook.History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 4Geography: Complete Chapter 4, Section 2 (The United States) in Eighth Grade Geography.OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Turn to p. 161. Read this page and the next. Then, using a photocopyof the map on p. 228, identify the places listed in the ‘Map-It’ section of p.162.Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-1928 1-10) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 3Listen to the String Quartet in C by Haydn.3/22/26, 6:37 PMPage 4 of 7
 Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Read for about 1 hour.3/22/26, 6:37 PMPage 5 of 7
        Thursday, March 26, 2026Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 1Lesson 65Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 4Take Religion Test I, available in the syllabus resources (here).English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 1Do workbook pages 128-129. (T.E. pages 254-257.)Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 25, Day: 1Attend Class. Do the Lesson XXII Quia Games. Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 4Read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.2. Answer question 7.2a in your workbook.History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 5Read The Magna Charta.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 1Recite the whole passage each day this week. You may also write the section down in your poetry notebook, and even illustrate it.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 4Read for about 1 hour.3/22/26, 6:37 PMPage 6 of 7
    
    Friday, March 27, 2026Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 2Lesson 66English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 2Go over workbook page 130 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 132-134. (T.E. pages 258-267.)Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 1Begin Lesson 9. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 9. Then look at the Lesson 9 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 25, Day: 2Lesson 23. Review sentence order in Latin (lesson VIII). Read the twenty-third lesson. Oral Drill: conjugate “sum,” “eram,” “ero,”“possum,” “poteram,” and “potero.” Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:sum, es, est, sumus, estis, sunteram, eras, erat, eramus, eratis, erantero, eris, erit, erimus, eritis, eruntpossum, potes, potest, possumus, potestis, possuntpoteram, poteras, poterat, poteramus, poteratis, poterantpotero, poteris, poterit, poterimus, poteritis, poteruntHistory & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 1Read The Magna Charta.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 2Recite and also try to render the first four lines in prose. What is Portia saying?Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 4Read Unit 4, Chapter 14, Anthony van Dyck.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 5Read for about 1 hour.3/22/26, 6:37 PMPage 7 of 7

blocks.subject: Algebra 1/2
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 63

blocks.subject: Religion 8
blocks.assignment_text: Work for 5 minutes reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Also review any of the questions for this year thatyou find you need to work on. Read Chapter 16 of Our Life in the Church.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Go over workbook page 121 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 122-123. (T.E. pages 243-247.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Mini Review Lessons 4-6, Selection 30: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Write sentences using the Lesson 8 Key words. You can put each word in a separate sentence, but if you use more than one word inthe same sentence that is even better. Make sure your sentences are intelligible.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: Science 7
blocks.assignment_text: Do some Nature Journaling.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read pp. 187-197 in The Old World and America. Do the Objective Tests, giving the source of your answer. If the answer comes outof the text, give the page number on which it is found. If you learned the answer from a different source, cite that source. Then dofive of the Questions that Make You Think and three of the Questions that Test Your Character on pp. 195-196. Remember, eachanswer should be a short paragraph that is three or more sentences long and should include the page number where you found theinformation. [Please note that this is an assessment assignment. Tips on completing these questions are available to parents in thesyllabus resources: Old World & America Questions - Tips and Instructions for Assessments.]Assessment Assignment:Week 16, Day 2, Questions That Make You Think & Questions That Test Your Character (pp. 195-196)

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Optional: Do Art Pad #13, Baroque Garland Project, Part 1.

blocks.subject: Reading 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Algebra 1/2
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 64

blocks.subject: Religion 8
blocks.assignment_text: Work for 5 minutes reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Also review any of the questions for this year thatyou find you need to work on. Write out the spiritual and corporal works of mercy. Discuss Chapter 16 of Our Life in the Church withyour teacher using the discussion questions in Chapter 16 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources).The exercises could be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions inChapter 16, Lessons 1-5 of the activity book as a guide.)

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do workbook page 124. Then go over workbook pages 125-127 with your teacher. (T.E. pages 248-253.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: find the errors and mark them in your book.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Write a single paragraph using as many of the Lessons 7 and 8 Key Words as you can. Make sure the paragraph is intelligible.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: Science 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.1.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Start reading The Magna Charta by James Daugherty (in print). Read it for the next week. If you find yourself with more time, try TheRed Keep and The Lost Baron by Allen French, or The Hidden Treasure of Glaston by Eleanor M. Jewett (all in print).

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.

blocks.subject: Reading 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Algebra 1/2
blocks.assignment_text: Test 15

blocks.subject: Religion 8
blocks.assignment_text: Work for 5 minutes reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Also review any of the questions for this year that youfind you need to work on. Add the spiritual and corporal works of mercy to your memory work.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Complete English 8: Semesterly Assessment 1 online.Assessment Assignment:English 8: Semesterly Assessment 1

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take a spelling test on the Lesson 7 and Lesson 8 Key Words.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Take Quiz 22.

blocks.subject: Science 7
blocks.assignment_text: Re-read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.1. Answer question 7.1 and The Big Idea in your workbook.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Geography: Complete Chapter 4, Section 2 (The United States) in Eighth Grade Geography.OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Turn to p. 161. Read this page and the next. Then, using a photocopyof the map on p. 228, identify the places listed in the ‘Map-It' section of p.162.

blocks.subject: Music 8
blocks.assignment_text: Listen to the String Quartet in C by Haydn.

blocks.subject: Reading 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Algebra 1/2
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 65

blocks.subject: Religion 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take Religion Test I, available in the syllabus resources (here).

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do workbook pages 128-129. (T.E. pages 254-257.)

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Attend Class. Do the Lesson XXII Quia Games.

blocks.subject: Science 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.2. Answer question 7.2a in your workbook.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read The Magna Charta.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Recite the whole passage each day this week. You may also write the section down in your poetry notebook, and even illustrate it.

blocks.subject: Reading 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Algebra 1/2
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 66

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Go over workbook page 130 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 132-134. (T.E. pages 258-267.)

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Begin Lesson 9. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 9. Then look at the Lesson 9 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 23. Review sentence order in Latin (lesson VIII). Read the twenty-third lesson. Oral Drill: conjugate "sum," "eram," "ero,""possum," "poteram," and "potero." Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:sum, es, est, sumus, estis, sunteram, eras, erat, eramus, eratis, erantero, eris, erit, erimus, eritis, eruntpossum, potes, potest, possumus, potestis, possuntpoteram, poteras, poterat, poteramus, poteratis, poterantpotero, poteris, poterit, poterimus, poteritis, poterunt

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read The Magna Charta.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Recite and also try to render the first four lines in prose. What is Portia saying?

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Unit 4, Chapter 14, Anthony van Dyck.

blocks.subject: Reading 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

Joseph.raw_text:
Weekly List for Joseph McAdamsMonday, March 23, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 2Lesson 56Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 13, Day: 2Discuss the questions for Chapter 15. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What question did Paul and Barnabas go to Jerusalem to discuss?2. Who said, “Why then do you now try to test God by putting on the neck of the disciples a yoke which neither our fathers norwe have been able to bear?”3. Which of the following restrictions did the council decide were necessary?CircumcisionAbstaining from bloodNot marrying foreignersAbstaining from porkAbstaining from things offered to idolsRitual hand washingAbstaining from anything strangledAvoiding immoralityReview your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 8, Day: 3Do workbook pages 62-63. (T.E. pages 120-123.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 13, Day: 2Lesson 6, Selection 27: find the errors and mark them in your book.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 19, Day: 3Lesson 17. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 5Take a spelling test on the Lesson 8 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 2Read pp. 78-80 (Learning, Medicine), and pp. 85-87 (Who was Who in Ancient Greece) in The Greeks. blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 56

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 15. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What question did Paul and Barnabas go to Jerusalem to discuss?2. Who said, "Why then do you now try to test God by putting on the neck of the disciples a yoke which neither our fathers norwe have been able to bear?"3. Which of the following restrictions did the council decide were necessary?CircumcisionAbstaining from bloodNot marrying foreignersAbstaining from porkAbstaining from things offered to idolsRitual hand washingAbstaining from anything strangledAvoiding immoralityReview your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do workbook pages 62-63. (T.E. pages 120-123.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 6, Selection 27: find the errors and mark them in your book.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 17. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take a spelling test on the Lesson 8 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read pp. 78-80 (Learning, Medicine), and pp. 85-87 (Who was Who in Ancient Greece) in The Greeks. Start Archimedes and theDoor of Science, reading about 30 pages.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Read Geology — Chapter 15: Section 15.1 and 15.2. Answer the questions in your workbook.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.

blocks.subject: Music 7
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Review for Unit 3.

blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 16, or write out your answers.1. Why did Paul circumcise Timothy?2. In what two ways did the churches improve?3. How did Paul decide what cities to travel to?4. Who insisted that Paul stay in her house in Macedonia?5. Why did the masters of the possessed girl bring Paul and Silas before the magistrates?6. What did they accuse Paul and Silas of?7. How were they freed from their imprisonment?8. Why did the guard want to kill himself?9. What did Paul want to be done before he would leave the prison?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Chapter 17 of Acts. Also, start working on two new Friendly Defender cards. Pick one card from each of the followingcategories: Holy Orders and Mary. Read the cards and think about the issues. Start to memorize the verses.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Go over workbook pages 69 and 71 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 70 and 72. (T.E. pages 130-137.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Mini Review Lessons 4-6, Selection 29: find the errors and mark them in your book.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 18. Review the second declension (lesson XVII). Read the eighteenth lesson. Make flashcards for new vocabulary. Oral Drill:decline "servus, servi" and "donum, doni." Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:servus, servi, servo, servum, servo, servi, servorum, servis, servos, servisdonum, doni, dono, donum, dono, dona, donorum, donis, dona, donis

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take a spelling test on the Lesson 7 and Lesson 8 Key Words.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read 30 pages, and take a test on the map study you have been doing. Take one of the photocopies of your original map and, withoutlooking, try to locate the following: Aegean Sea, Mediterranean Sea, Ionian Sea, Mt. Olympus, Athens, Crete, Hellespont, Thebes,Ithaca, Sparta, Troy (Ilium), Thessaly, Mycenae, Pylos, Delphi, Thermopylae, Marathon, Plataea, Salamis, Corinth, Olympia.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Review of Geology — Chapter 15 in your workbook.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Optional: Do Art Pad #14, Baroque Garland Project, Part 2.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

Weekly List for Joseph McAdams
Monday, March 23, 2026
Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 56
Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles)Discuss the questions for Chapter 15. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What question did Paul and Barnabas go to Jerusalem to discuss?2. Who said, "Why then do you now try to test God by putting on the neck of the disciples a yoke which neither our fathers norwe have been able to bear?"3. Which of the following restrictions did the council decide were necessary?CircumcisionAbstaining from bloodNot marrying foreignersAbstaining from porkAbstaining from things offered to idolsRitual hand washingAbstaining from anything strangledAvoiding immoralityReview your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do workbook pages 62-63. (T.E. pages 120-123.)

History & Geog 7 (Old World & America)Read pp. 78-80 (Learning, Medicine), and pp. 85-87 (Who was Who in Ancient Greece) in The Greeks. Start Archimedes and theDoor of Science, reading about 30 pages. 
 
Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 6)Read Geology — Chapter 15: Section 15.1 and 15.2. Answer the questions in your workbook.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.
Music 7 (Alfred's Essentials of Music)Do the Review for Unit 3.
Reading 7 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour.

Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles)Read Chapter 16 of Acts. Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards youworked on previously.

Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 6, Selection 27: write the selection correctly.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Lesson 17. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

History & Geog 7 (Old World & America)Read 30 pages of Archimedes and the Door of Science.

Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles)Discuss the questions for Chapter 16, or write out your answers.1. Why did Paul circumcise Timothy?2. In what two ways did the churches improve?3. How did Paul decide what cities to travel to?4. Who insisted that Paul stay in her house in Macedonia?5. Why did the masters of the possessed girl bring Paul and Silas before the magistrates?6. What did they accuse Paul and Silas of?7. How were they freed from their imprisonment?8. Why did the guard want to kill himself?9. What did Paul want to be done before he would leave the prison?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.
